name: Enterprise-Grade Critical Fixes
description: Foundation: Security, reliability, compliance. Must complete before anything else.

sharedRules.noEmojis: No emojis in code, comments, UI, logs, or documentation
sharedRules.featureBasedModules: Organize code by feature, not by layer
sharedRules.researchFirst: Use Context7 MCP + Brave Search before ANY implementation. Document findings.
sharedRules.workflowEnforced: Commit → Push → Update JSON → Write Report. In that order.
sharedRules.testBeforeComplete: Run full test suite before marking step complete. All tests must pass.
sharedRules.fullyImplemented: No placeholders, TODOs, or incomplete code. Everything must work.
sharedRules.modularArchitecture: Keep files under 250 lines. Split when needed.
sharedRules.seoOptimized: All frontend pages must have proper meta tags, structured data, and semantic HTML
sharedRules.apiVersioning: All APIs must be versioned (v1, v2) with backward compatibility policy

workflow: 1. Research: Use Context7 MCP (library docs) + Brave Search (best practices)
5. Run all tests: npm run test:all (or equivalent)
7. Commit with conventional commit: feat(phase{}): step Y - title
9. Update this phase{}.json with completion data
11. Mark step as completed in JSON
12. Create Pull Request, get review, merge

steps.title: Enable PostgreSQL RLS on All Tenant Tables
steps.objective: Complete implementation of Enable PostgreSQL RLS on All Tenant Tables with production-ready quality, following all mandatory rules.

requiredSearches: Enable PostgreSQL RLS on All Tenant Tables implementation examples
questions: What are the security considerations for ['enable', 'postgresql']?
How to test ['enable', 'postgresql'] effectively?
What are common pitfalls in ['enable', 'postgresql'] implementation?

step1.description: Research PostgreSQL RLS and verify database setup
tasks: Connect to PostgreSQL: docker exec nextmavens-research-db psql -U flow -d nextmavens_research
Check existing tables to understand current schema
Research RLS policies: USING vs WITH CHECK clauses
Copy the provided schema.prisma with all tenant models
Review schema: ensure all org_id columns are present on tenant tables
Verify relationship mapping (org -> instances -> messages, etc.)
- ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY for 15 tables
- CREATE POLICY admin_bypass for SUPER_ADMIN (USING true)
- SET app.current_org = orgId for regular users
- Throw 403 if user not in org

step5.description: Create Prisma client singleton and database utilities
tasks: Add verifyDatabaseSetup() to check RLS on startup
- Context filtering works (org 1 sees only org 1 data)
Include cleanup() function for test isolation
npx prisma migrate dev --name init (creates tables)
npx prisma db push (applies RLS migration)
All tests MUST pass before marking complete
- RLS policies created (count: 2 per table = 30 policies)
- Decisions: Why RLS, superseding application-level checks
- Challenges: Any issues with policies, session variables

tests: npm run test:rls passes (all integration tests)

Manual verification: 
  - Set app.current_org = 'org-uuid'
  - SELECT * FROM whatsapp_messages
  - Confirm only org's rows return
manuallyVerify: Connect to database: docker exec nextmavens-research-db psql -U flow -d nextmavens_research
Run: SELECT relname, relrowsecurity FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'whatsapp_messages'
Should show rls_enabled = true
Run: \dp whatsapp_messages to list policies
Should show admin_bypass and tenant_isolation policies
acceptanceCriteria: All 15 tenant tables have RLS enabled
30 policies created (2 per table: admin bypass + tenant isolation)

deliverables: All tests passing: npm run test:rls

reportTemplate.summary: Brief summary of what was implemented for Enable PostgreSQL RLS on All Tenant Tables
challenges: Any blockers encountered and how they were resolved

step1.description: Set up BullMQ queue workers and job processing system
details: Follow shared rules: no emojis, max 250 lines/file, primary colors only

Ensure all code is type-safe with TypeScript
Add proper error handling and logging

step2.description: Complete Implement BullMQ Message Queue System implementation with validation and error handling
details: Implement full CRUD operations if applicable

step3.description: Test and validate Implement BullMQ Message Queue System thoroughly
details: Run all tests: npm run test:all
Check code coverage > 90% for critical paths
Perform manual testing of all scenarios

tests: Integration tests for critical paths pass

Security controls are effective (RLS, rate limiting, etc.)
